name: Rutledge v. United States
summary: Held that a continuing criminal enterprise conviction and a lesser-included drug conspiracy conviction could not both stand because Congress had not clearly authorized cumulative punishment, despite both statutes containing mandatory and non-suspendable sentences.
significance: Key precedent showing that 'notwithstanding any other provision of law' language and mandatory minimum provisions in overlapping drug statutes were insufficient to constitute a clear indication of congressional intent to authorize cumulative punishment.
